---
title: "RP 25: Making New Friends in Recovery"
format: html
---
# RP 25: Making New Friends in Recovery

Social environment is one of the strongest predictors of recovery outcomes. Building new, supportive relationships can reduce isolation, normalize sober living, and increase accountability. This takes time and often feels uncomfortable at first.

A helpful approach is to focus on **shared activities** rather than immediate closeness. Regular contact around healthy routines often becomes friendship naturally.

Social network change is a high-impact recovery intervention. Increasing contact with pro-recovery peers reduces exposure to drinking norms and strengthens accountability during vulnerable moments.

## Connection Builder
| Place to meet supportive people | First step I can take | Date |
|---|---|---|
| Mutual-help group |  |  |
| Volunteer setting |  |  |
| Hobby/fitness/community class |  |  |

::: {.callout-note}
### Reflection and action

As you complete this section, link each insight to one specific behavior.

Take at least one first step this week and record how it felt before and after.

Small repeatable actions are more useful than perfect one-time efforts.
:::
